data.title: Enemies to friends? Mark Zuckerberg wants 'active role' in Trump's tech policies
data.description:
Meta (NASDAQ:META) CEO Mark Zuckerberg is seeking an "active role" in tech policy discussions with the incoming U.S. administration, indicating that relations between the executive and president-elect Donald Trump may be improving.

Zuckerberg is keen to play "an active role in the debates that any administration needs to have about maintaining America's leadership in the technological sphere," Nick Clegg, Meta's (META) global affairs president, told the media.

This "is tremendously important given all the geostrategic uncertainties around the world, and particularly the pivotal role that AI will play," Clegg added.

Trump has largely been critical of Zuckerberg, after being banned from Facebook and Instagram in 2021 for praising his supporters who participated in the January 6 Capitol attack.

Trump repeatedly claimed that Meta (META) censors conservative views on its social media platforms. He even warned that Zuckerberg would "spend the rest of his life in prison" if he interfered in the 2024 presidential election.

Zuckerberg in August committed to remaining neutral this election cycle. Trump later softened his stance, saying it was "nice" that Zuckerberg was "staying out of the election."

Trump even invited Zuckerberg to dine at the Mar-a-Lago resort in Florida last week, but it's unclear what they discussed.

In addition to his pledge to stay neutral during the election cycle, Zuckerberg revealed that the Biden administration pressured Meta (META) to censor COVID-19 content.

Clegg said in hindsight, Meta (META) "overdid it a bit" when it came to moderating COVID-related content. "We're acutely aware because users quite rightly raised their voice and complained that we sometimes over-enforce."

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Okta tops Q3 estimates; initiates Q4 and raises FY24 outlook below estimates
Spain’s services PMI falls in November, weaker growth linked to adverse impact of flooding
South Korea stocks fall amid political chaos after martial law reversed
data.description:
South Korean stocks came under pressure on Wednesday after opposition parties submitted a motion to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived declaration of martial law.

The benchmark Kospi (KOSPI) index fell as much as 2.3% and the Kosdaq slid 2%. Seoul-listed shares of Samsung Electronics, the country's largest company, dropped 3% before recovering slightly to close 1.1% lower. Battery-maker LG Energy Solution and automaker Hyundai Motor fell over 2% each.

South Korea's parliament, with 190 of its 300 members present, passed a motion early on Wednesday to lift the martial law that was declared Tuesday night. The speaker of parliament declared the martial law announcement invalid.

South Korea's won (KRW:USD) slowly started recovering on Wednesday, a day after falling dramatically against the U.S. dollar on the president's surprise declaration.

The martial law announcement triggered widespread protests and a political standoff as lawmakers opposed the move, escalating tensions in Asia's third-largest economy.

In response, South Korea's finance ministry assured markets its readiness to inject "unlimited liquidity" to ensure stability if required.

data.title: Synovus declares $0.38 dividend
data.description:
• Synovus (NYSE:SNV) declares $0.38/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.

data.title: Gaotu Techedu Non-GAAP EPS of -RMB 1.78, revenue of RMB 1.21B; initiates Q4 outlook
data.description:
• Gaotu Techedu press release (NYSE:GOTU): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of -RMB 1.78.
  • Revenue of RMB 1.21B (+53.3% Y/Y).
  • Gross billings were RMB1,069.2 million, increased by 67.2% from RMB639.3 million in the same period of 2023.
  • Net operating cash outflow was RMB714.4 million, compared with net operating cash outflow of RMB209.9 million in the same period of 2023.
  • As of September 30, 2024, the company had cash and cash equivalents, restricted cash, short-term and long-term investments of RMB3,310.0 million in aggregate, compared with a total of RMB3,953.5 million as of December 31, 2023.

  • Business Outlook: Based on the company's current estimates, total net revenues for the fourth quarter of 2024 are expected to be between RMB1,288 million and RMB1,308 million, representing an increase of 69.2% to 71.9% on a year-over-year basis.
